extraction.one_line_summary: KEMNA Leonard won from 14-man breakaway after LOPEZ Juan Pedro attacked at climb base, took sprint bonuses, then negotiated for jersey while yielding stage.
tactical_patterns: Lodeweges Trek abandoned Maglia Rosa defense early, sending Oldani to break while leader went to gruppetto, prioritizing GC energy.
Lopez attacked at climb base specifically to collect intermediate sprint bonuses before breakaway catch, pre-planning jersey math.
Bahrain and Ineos controlled despite 12-minute gap, revealing neither team trusted their sprinters enough to let break succeed.
Bora placed three riders in GC group plus stage winner Kemna forward, showing superior depth distribution tactics.

rider_signals.LOPEZ Juan Pedro: positive: GC debut rider sophisticated enough to pre-calculate bonus seconds and negotiate stage/jersey split with breakaway companion
rider_signals.DUMOULIN Tom: negative: first major GC name dropped inside 15km on moderate summit finish, suggesting worse form than Ineos anticipated
rider_signals.NIBALI Vincenzo: negative: distanced with 5km remaining on home island climb, confirming retirement-track form rather than GC contention
rider_signals.CARAPAZ Richard: neutral: attempted to steal seconds in final meters while favorites group stayed together, showing alertness without real superiority
rider_signals.PORTE Richie: negative: lost time to main GC group on moderate climb, early signal of weakness before mountains intensify
rider_signals.YATES Simon: positive: remained with favorites despite knee injury sustained earlier, suggesting incident won't compromise GC challenge
